items.title: Iran: Forewarnings and a Funeral, NATO, Le Pen
items.description: It’s been a week of shifting alliances, escalating tensions and political drama. NATO leaders gathered in Turkey for another pivotal summit, where President Trump arrived questioning the alliance’s loyalty before leaving in a markedly warmer mood, as attention also turned to his relationship with President Zelensky and the future of Western support for Ukraine. In the Middle East, Iran held days of public mourning for its late Supreme Leader while fresh exchanges with the United States cast further doubt over hopes for a lasting ceasefire. In France, Marine Le Pen's political fortunes were transformed by a landmark appeal court ruling that reignited her presidential ambitions, while on the football pitch Les Bleus moved a step closer to a third World Cup title as Kylian Mbappé forcefully responded to racist abuse from a Paraguayan senator.

items.title: Behind Khamenei’s funeral, Iran’s bid to show strength
items.description: Ahou Koutchesfahani, scholar in international relations and Iran expert, analyses the funeral ceremonies for Ali Khamenei as a carefully orchestrated display of power by the Islamic Republic. The regime is seeking to send two messages: to its own population, by showcasing its control and ability to mobilise large crowds, and to the international community, by reinforcing the image of Khamenei as an anti-imperialist figure who stood up to the United States. Through these ceremonies, Tehran is also attempting to strengthen its narrative and rally support beyond its borders.

items.title: Taiwan braces for Super Typhoon Bavi
items.description: Residents in northern Taiwan are preparing for Typhoon Bavi as authorities evacuate thousands of people, shut schools and businesses, and brace for torrential rain, flooding and winds of up to 155 km/h.The storm is expected to make landfall in southeast China on Saturday after already causing deadly landslides in the Philippines and widespread damage in Guam and the Northern Mariana Islands.

items.title: IMF confirms 3.5% growth forecast for Argentina in 2026
items.description: Multilateral lender’s chief Kristalina Georgieva to visit Buenos Aires this month; International Monetary Fund sees Argentina’s economy expanding 4% in 2027. Leer más

items.title: South Korean billionaire's risky bet pays off, as SK Hynix debuts in New York
Could football break away from FIFA and its World Cup?
items.description: FIFA’s actions at the World Cup, and closeness to Donald Trump, have put the organization under fire. Federations, fans, coaches and players have gone public with complaints, but is there a way out?

items.title: Argentina shuns markets for now, sees investment grade by 2031
items.description: President Javier Milei’s administration is targeting investment-grade status for Argentina by the end of a potential second term in 2031, announces Economy Minister Luis Caputo. Leer más

items.title: Creator of Cape Fear TV show probably first heard about it from The Simpsons
items.description: The 1957 book The Executioners has been made into two movies and an iconic episode of The Simpsons. It's now a TV show, starring Amy Adams, Patrick Wilson and Javier Bardem.

items.title: DR Congo says Ebola outbreak yet to peak as virus spreads to new areas
items.description: The Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DR Congo) remains in a "very active" phase and has yet to peak, health authorities said Thursday

items.title: Venezuela quake death toll rises to nearly 3,000
items.description: Eleven days after the devastating twin earthquakes, rescue teams are starting to wrap up searches for survivors while families still try to recover bodies of loved ones from the rubble. Leer más

items.title: Paris court hands monitor 18-month suspended sentence for assaulting preschooler in abuse scandal
items.description: A Paris court handed a school monitor an 18-month suspended prison sentence on Friday for sexually abusing a kindergarten-age child. It's the first such guilty verdict since the French capital was rocked by a wave of allegations of child sex abuse by non-teaching staff in Paris's public school system.

items.title: Nvidia supplier King Yuan Electronics to invest up to $1.4 billion in US facility
Watch: India and Australia Deepen Defence Ties: What It Means for the Indo-Pacific | Above the Fold | 09.07.2026
items.description: From Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s visit to Australia to the escalating conflict in West Asia, to new developments in the Sri Lanka prison clashes, we bring you the latest in this episode. We also look at the Navy’s Drishti-10 UAV that crashed near Gujarat, the controversy surrounding Argentina’s latest FIFA World Cup win, and other top national and international stories. We bring you your complete news roundup.

items.title: NBA invites foreign, domestic observers to monitor its election, amid controversies
items.description: The NBA election is slated to be held electronically on 20 July. The post NBA invites foreign, domestic observers to monitor its election, amid controversies appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: 'We didn't want to go home' – Messi on Argentina's dramatic comeback
items.description: Skipper on his emotions following victory over Egypt: "It was a feeling of pure happiness and relief." Leer más

items.title: Egypt's wrath after World Cup exit: 'The match was rigged'
items.description: Egypt were left seething by their narrow and dramatic round-of-16 defeat against Argentina. Coach Hossam Hassan accused the referee of favoring the defending World Cup champions.

items.title: Attacks on Kyiv ahead of NATO summit: What is Putin's goal?
items.description: Kyiv suffered heavy Russian strikes ahead of the NATO summit in Ankara. Experts say Russian President Vladimir Putin is trying to send a message.

items.title: Heat-resistant housing: How we need to adapt our homes
items.description: In Germany, it is time to rethink and rebuild. Many apartments were not designed for heat waves. Fortunately, there are ways to adapt existing buildings to the changing climate — but air conditioning isn't the best.

items.title: Toyota eyes lunar rover powered by regenerative fuel-cell tech
items.description: Toyota has teamed up with JAXA since 2019 to develop the manned lunar rover — which it dubbed the Lunar Cruiser — that they hope can be put on the moon in 2029.

items.title: Spain: Nine injured in fourth San Fermín bull run in Pamplona
items.description: The fourth San Fermin bull run was once again a fast and dangerous dash along the cobbled streets of Pamplona.

items.title: Opposition rallies against Tshisekedi's third-term push
items.description: Political tensions are rising in the Democratic Republic of Congo as opposition leaders demand President Félix Tshisekedi's resignation and call for nationwide protests over proposed constitutional changes that could extend his time in power.

items.title: Assassin's Creed: Why pop culture is hooked to pirates
items.description: Pirates have captured our imagination for centuries. With "Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced," gamers can now head off on another pirate adventure. How has our concept of pirates evolved with pop culture?

items.title: More stars or stripes?
items.description: Within a decade of the Civil War, the US economy had overtaken Great Britain and the entire British Empire by around 1895 – from that point onward, it was more a question of the US impact on world history than the reverse. Leer más

items.title: China announces temporary export ban on helium
items.description: China imposes a temporary helium export ban amid military tensions, aiming to prevent domestic shortages critical for chip manufacturing

items.title: Mbappe hits back at racist insults from Paraguay senator
items.description: A Paraguayan lawmaker has sparked outrage with racist insults directed at footballer Kylian Mbappe. The French FA says it will take legal action, and President Emmanuel Macron has expressed support for the player.

items.title: Amnesty urges investigating Israeli attacks on Lebanon as ‘war crimes’
items.description: Amnesty said it reached out to Israeli authorities, who said that some of the attacks "were carried out against Hezbollah military objectives", while others were "referred for examination".

items.title: Syria welcomes U.S. announcement of removal from blacklist
items.description: "A dark page in Syria's history has been turned," Foreign Minister Asaad al-Shaibani wrote on X

items.title: Andy Burnham on the verge of becoming U.K. PM
items.description: Burnham, who is from Labour’s ‘soft left’ and was elected three times to run Manchester, won a byelection in Makerfield on June 18 in order to return to Westminster, as support for Keir Starmer within his party continued to evaporate.

items.title: Luis Caputo: Argentina has secured funds for 2026 debt payments
items.description: Economy Minister Luis Caputo says Milei government has secured enough funds to meet this year's external debt obligations; Administration open to international markets to refinance future maturities, but not in a rush. Leer más

items.title: India news: Prime Minister Modi embarks on maiden trip to New Zealand
items.description: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi is concluding a tri-country tour with a visit to New Zealand. On his docket are bilateral talks on trade, commerce and defense. DW has more.

items.title: Outback towns welcome grey nomads after slow start to tourism season
items.description: Grey nomads are giving a much-needed boost to outback Queensland towns over the winter tourism season, but locals say more visitors are needed to fuel economic growth.

items.title: Egypt were 'cheated' in controversial World Cup exit to Argentina, says coach
items.description: "Perhaps they wanted to keep the world champions in the competition. Perhaps they wanted Messi to stay in the running," says Egypt coach Hossam Hassan after dramatic loss to Argentina. Leer más

items.title: Argentina's gruelling World Cup schedule a concern for Scaloni
items.description: Leandro Paredes, Nicolás Tagliafico and Julián Álvarez set to return to starting line-up for clash against Egypt. Leer más

items.title: Death toll in Southern Spain wildfire climbs to 12
items.description: "The number of people who died in the fire in Los Gallardos has risen to 12 after the confirmation of six more deaths," the regional government of Andalusia said in a statement

items.title: What's next for Iran after the funeral of their late supreme leader?
items.description: The BBC's chief international correspondent looks at how Iran's new leadership will respond to several issues affecting the country.

items.title: India, Australia strike deal on uranium exports, deepen ties across nuclear and maritime sectors
items.description: The agreement on civil nuclear energy to facilitate the commercial supply of uranium from Australia to India to fuel New Delhi's nuclear power projects came nearly 12 years after the two countries inked a historic civil nuclear cooperation pact

items.title: Mali: Water returns to Bamako after sabotage disrupts power supply
items.description: Water was back on in Bamako on Wednesday, following sabotage to an electrical supply line that left much of the Malian capital without power for several days.

items.title: Notes from a NATO Summit: An Estonian Perspective
items.description: As the week draws to a close following the NATO summit in Ankara, a clearer picture is emerging of what was happening behind closed doors inside the Presidential Compound as the alliance's 32 leaders met. Among the revelations, reports from The Wall Street Journal and The Times of Israel suggest President Trump's team was rapidly adjusting plans amid what appeared to be an ongoing Iranian assassination threat. Away from the main sessions, the summit was also shaped by a series of side meetings, including talks between the Baltic leaders and President Zelensky, while President Erdogan's controversial gifts to visiting leaders – ceremonial pistols presented with ammunition enclosed – prompted an unexpected diplomatic and ethical dilemma over whether to accept them. France 24's Gavin Lee spoke to Joakim Klementi, European correspondent for Estonian Public Broadcasting, ERR.

items.title: Syria says it arrested suspects behind the Damascus bombings during Macron’s visit
items.description: Last week, an explosive device detonated in a cafe near Damascus’ main judicial complex, killing at least 10 people and wounding more than 20

items.title: Paraguay condems own senator for racist remarks against Mbappé
items.description: The Paraguayan Senate passed a motion against Senator Celeste Amarilla after her racist remarks against France star Kylian Mbappé, while the Senator insulted the player again during the Senate session Wednesday

items.title: Adrenaline junkie 89yo skydives to celebrate his birthday
items.description: Donald isn't alone in chasing thrills at an older age, with data from SkyDive Australia showing more than 14,000 people over 50 have dived since 2023.

items.title: Germany: Bundestag passes controversial healthcare reform
items.description: The cost-cutting package has passed Germany's parliament despite fierce criticism from the opposition. Meanwhile, Alexander Zverev has become the first German man to reach the Wimbledon final since 1995. DW has more.

items.title: Nissan management shake-up plans reported as sharks circle
items.description: Nissan is considering a change in leadership following poor results and the failure of a proposed merger with Honda, according to a report citing unnamed sources.

items.title: Argentina produce epic World Cup fightback to beat Egypt, reach quarters
items.description: Lionel Messi inspired Argentina to a stunning late comeback as the reigning champions beat Egypt 3-2 on Tuesday to reach the World Cup quarter-finals; Match swung by controversial VAR call against African side. Leer más

items.title: A 'well-known' risk caused Telstra's outage. Why did it get caught out?
items.description: Telstra faces an outage investigation as an expert says legal gaps may let the telco off the hook.
